_comment: P49 WS3: operational-URL provenance sidecar. Drift invariant 46 (check_url_provenance) requires every http(s):// literal in tools/**/*.py to be accounted for by exactly one of: (a) a host in source-registry.json (data/reference sources), (b) a base domain listed here (infrastructure/plumbing endpoints), or (c) an excluded-by-rule host (example/placeholder/localhost/schema namespace). This file covers bucket (b): endpoints the code calls that are NOT re-checkable data sources. Entries are BASE DOMAINS; the invariant matches a host if it equals or is a subdomain of a listed domain. Add a new endpoint here (with a reason) rather than letting an undeclared URL ship. Doc bibliographies (docs/**, planning JSONs) are out of scope by rule -- the guard scans executable code only.

allowed.reason: YouTube Data/Analytics API + Google OAuth token endpoint (www.googleapis.com, youtubeanalytics.googleapis.com, oauth2.googleapis.com)
YouTube short-link host used when building a video permalink
Instagram Graph API + profile surface (graph.instagram.com, www.instagram.com)
Pinterest API v5 + pin/profile surface (api.pinterest.com, www.pinterest.com)
TikTok share-link host used in a sample parse path
Google Cloud Console link shown in the wizard OAuth setup
US Census geocoder used by the offline jurisdiction geocoding seam
Wayback Machine snapshot fallback in fetch_resilient / freshness overlay (archive.org, web.archive.org)
raw.githubusercontent.com for the Homebrew install script and NREL openstudio-standards data files
GGML whisper.cpp model download host (local STT doctor)
Homebrew homepage shown in the macOS setup path
Node.js download link shown when the Microsoft 365 MCP needs Node
claude.ai web surface referenced in the wizard; also appears in secret-scan selftest fixtures
